raw_body:
Ahead of the AI & Big Data Expo at the San Jose McEnery Convention Center, May 18-19, we spoke to Jerome Gabryszewski, the company’s AI & Data Science Business Development Manager about AI, processing data for AI ingestion, and local versus cloud compute. The technology media is fond of quoting that data is ‘the new […]
The post HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: HP's AI & Data Science Business Development Manager, Jerome Gabryszewski, discussed the importance of data processing for AI ahead of the AI & Big Data Expo. He emphasized the ongoing debate between local and cloud computing for AI applications. With data being described as 'the new oil,' efficient data ingestion is crucial for enterprises. This conversation highlights the growing need for businesses to optimize their data strategies to leverage AI effectively.
why_it_matters: Enterprises need to process data efficiently to enhance their AI capabilities, impacting their competitive edge. This shift could lead to improved decision-making and operational efficiency.
The conversation around local versus cloud computing signifies a broader trend where companies are reassessing their data infrastructure to stay relevant in an AI-driven market.

lenses.eli12: Jerome Gabryszewski from HP spoke about how important it is for businesses to handle their data properly for AI. He pointed out that companies are debating whether to use local servers or cloud services for their AI needs. This matters because better data processing can help businesses make smarter choices and work more efficiently, which can affect everyone in the economy.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, Gabryszewski's insights underline the need to focus on efficient data processing for AI applications. Choosing between local and cloud computing could impact costs and operational efficiency. This means that understanding data ingestion methods is crucial for creating competitive products that meet user demands.
lenses.engineer: From a technical standpoint, Gabryszewski's remarks highlight the importance of optimizing data ingestion methods for AI applications. The choice between local and cloud computing can significantly affect processing speed and resource allocation. Engineers should consider how these decisions impact model performance and scalability, especially as enterprises increasingly rely on data-driven insights.
